prompt:
Asset-sync service, media team. GraphDriveClient (metadata reads against Microsoft Graph v1.0 drives) works fine and must keep working. What does NOT work is how we ship big renders into SharePoint: someone PUTs the whole 800MB to the /content endpoint and when the VPN hiccups at 97% we start over from zero. Marketing is furious. I need a resumable upload-session uploader as a new class, LargeFileUploader, next to the existing client.

Non-negotiable first step, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Microsoft Graph docs on learn.microsoft.com — the v1.0 driveItem: createUploadSession reference and the uploadSession resource page. Confirm the session-creation call, the Content-Range PUT protocol and its fragment-size law (the 320 KiB thing), what nextExpectedRanges means, the 416 and 404 recovery stories, and the rule about the Authorization header on the upload URL — that last one bit us in a prototype. docs/contract.json pins our understanding (provenance in docs/official_sources.json); both protected. If the live docs disagree with the fixture, report the conflict in your summary and do not touch fixtures or tests. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

The new class (plain java.net.http like the rest of the codebase, no dependencies):

new LargeFileUploader(client, fragmentSize, delaySeconds, maxRetries) — client is the existing GraphDriveClient (reuse its base URL/token/transport), delaySeconds is a java.util.function.LongConsumer the tests inject (never Thread.sleep when provided), maxRetries like 3.

DriveItem upload(driveId, parentItemId, fileName, byte[] content, conflictBehavior):
- Validate fragmentSize up front: positive, a multiple of 320 KiB (327,680 bytes), under 60 MiB — otherwise IllegalArgumentException before ANY network traffic.
- POST .../items/{parentItemId}:/{fileName}:/createUploadSession with the item.@microsoft.graph.conflictBehavior body (item.name must match the path segment). Bearer auth here and ONLY here.
- PUT fragments to the returned uploadUrl sequentially with exact Content-Range bytes {first}-{last}/{total} headers. The upload URL is preauthenticated — sending our bearer token to it is a bug and the fake 401s it.
- 202 → next fragment. Final fragment answers 200/201 with the finished driveItem — return it (id/name/size).
- 5xx on a fragment: wait 1, 2, 4, ... seconds via the injected delay (consecutive-failure exponential backoff), re-PUT the SAME fragment, at most maxRetries times, then let the decoded GraphApiException fly.
- 416 means the server already has those bytes (classic after an ambiguous failure): not an error — GET the uploadUrl for status and resume from the first missing range in nextExpectedRanges.
- 404 from the upload URL means the session evaporated (they expire): create ONE fresh session and re-upload from byte 0; a second 404 is terminal.
- Graph errors decode through the usual envelope into GraphApiException (statusCode/errorCode/message, never the token).

java TestMain.java runs the acceptance harness (loopback fake drive, dummy token) — existing client behavior is covered there too and must stay green. TestMain.java and docs/ are protected.

research.product: Microsoft Graph OneDrive/SharePoint large-file upload sessions
research.interface: Microsoft Graph REST API v1.0, driveItem: createUploadSession + uploadSession PUT protocol

official_sources.title: driveItem: createUploadSession - Microsoft Graph v1.0
official_sources.used_for: POST /drives/{driveId}/items/{parentItemId}:/{fileName}:/createUploadSession request with item.@microsoft.graph.conflictBehavior body; uploadUrl/expirationDateTime response; fragment PUTs with Content-Length and Content-Range 'bytes {first}-{last}/{total}'; sequential order requirement; 320 KiB (327,680-byte) fragment multiple rule and sub-60 MiB request cap; 202 + nextExpectedRanges intermediate responses; final 200/201 with the driveItem; 416 on already-received ranges with status query recovery; GET uploadUrl to resume; 404 = session gone, restart the entire upload; 5xx retry-with-backoff best practice; no Authorization header on the preauthenticated uploadUrl; DELETE to cancel

official_sources.title: uploadSession resource type - Microsoft Graph v1.0
official_sources.used_for: uploadSession property semantics: uploadUrl accepts the byte-range PUTs; expirationDateTime is UTC and extends with every fragment; nextExpectedRanges is a collection of zero-indexed 'start-end'/'start-' strings listing ranges the server is still missing

research.language_rationale: The uploader extends an existing Java asset-sync service that already speaks Graph REST through java.net.http; the upload-session protocol is plain HTTP with exact header semantics (Content-Range, no-auth PUTs), which the stdlib HttpClient pins more faithfully than the Graph Java SDK's LargeFileUploadTask abstraction.
